master_row.title: Relation: π,Δ_topological/N_max
master_row.canonical_statement: llapse at the TZP. Mathematically, the particle-specific entanglement protocol is captured by an overarching Unified Entanglement Meta-Theorem. One formulation (from the Ω-protocol) states: “The $Ω[T^2 \times T^2, \pi, \text{DAAN}]$ kissing protocol achieves complete decoherence suppression for all particle–neural entanglement”. The proof employs a combination of Lindblad spectrum analysis and topological gap protection. We derive that the Liouvillian of the open system has a degenerate zero eigenvalue corresponding to a protected steady state, and a spectral gap $\lambda_{1}$ on the order of $\pi,\Delta_{\text{topological}}/N_{\max}$ (with $N_{\max}$ the maximum kissing number) separates this steady state from excited decohering modes. Simultaneously, adding a $\pi$-periodic sphere-packing symmetry creates an extensive stabilizer group that yields a topological error threshold $p_{\text{th}} = 1 - 1/\pi \approx 0.682$ – meaning error rates below $~31.8%$ can be corrected, a remarkably high threshold.
